evolution.note: Evolutionary invariants computed for lengths <= 8; structural invariants for all lengths <= 10.

Constant.description: All-Good or all-Bad. Worst possible autocorrelation; trivially recognised but maximally confusable with any shift of itself.

Alternating.description: Strict period-2 (GBGB.../BGBG...). Highly self-overlapping; cheap to describe, weak as a distinguishing signal.

Barker.description: Peak autocorrelation sidelobe <= 1 -- the rare optimal low-autocorrelation codes. Maximally resistant to out-of-phase mimicry. Conjectured to stop at length 13.

Unbordered.description: Bifix-free: no proper prefix equals a suffix. No partial self-echo, so hard to desynchronise or partially impersonate.

Self-similar.description: Low realized complexity -- small minimal automaton and short LFSR relative to length (constant-free but structurally compressible, Thue-Morse-like).

Generic.description: The incompressible bulk: high-complexity prefixes with no special autocorrelation, bordering, or low-complexity structure.
